blurb: The Cobb County UGA Extension is putting on a free summer cooking demo at Freeman Poole Senior Center on Friday, led by Zoe S. The focus is fresh, easy recipes built around summer produce, the kind of thing that actually changes how you think about what's in season right now. It's a quick hour at noon. If you like cooking and want some new ideas for the back half of summer, this is a low-effort way to get them.
scoring_notes: Free, local, and food-focused plays reasonably well with the educated, higher-income East Cobb audience who tend to care about quality food and cooking. Midday Friday and senior center location limits reach. The Smyrna location is slightly outside the core East Cobb area. Included as a filler option.

blurb: Deputy Watts from the Cobb County Sheriff's Office is presenting on situational awareness and personal security Monday morning at the Senior Wellness Center in Marietta. It's a free hour, and the kind of practical information that applies to everyone regardless of age. If you've got a parent who'd benefit from hearing this from an actual law enforcement professional, or you just want the refresher yourself, it's right up the road and costs nothing.
scoring_notes: Similar profile to the Coffee with a Cop entry. Skews toward seniors and empty nesters. Practical community safety content is relevant to a high-homeownership East Cobb audience. Midmorning timing on a Monday limits the working adult segment. Included as a filler option but not a strong featured event candidate.

venue: Tim D. Lee Senior Center, Marietta
blurb: Officer Justice from the Cobb County Police Department's Community Affairs division is stopping by Tim D. Lee Senior Center on Friday morning to talk personal safety. It's an open conversation, free, and the kind of thing that's easy to walk away from feeling like you actually learned something useful. If you've got a parent in the area or you're just someone who likes knowing what's going on in the community, this one's worth the hour.
scoring_notes: Skews toward the senior and empty nester end of the audience. Midday Friday limits working adults. Included because community safety is a genuine interest in a high-homeownership neighborhood like East Cobb, and the personal safety topic has broad relevance. Lower scores across the board due to niche timing and audience.

venue: Tim D. Lee Senior Center, Marietta
blurb: Yes, it's July. Yes, this is a Christmas card workshop. And honestly? Getting ahead of the holiday card pile by six months is the most on-brand East Cobb move imaginable. The Tim D. Lee Senior Center is running a 2.5-hour class on Tuesday where you'll make three cards from kits that have everything precut and ready to go. Some card-making experience is helpful but not required. If you've got a creative streak and a habit of scrambling in December, show up in July instead.
scoring_notes: Skews toward the empty nester and senior end of the East Cobb audience. The timing (midday Tuesday) limits working adults. Included for uniqueness and the niche appeal of the Christmas-in-July hook, which is genuinely fun and specific. Not a top pick for the full audience, but serves a real segment of the readership.

event_name: Naturalist Club: Reptiles of Cobb County
blurb: If you've got a curious kid at home this summer (or you're just the kind of adult who wants to know what's actually living in your backyard), the Cobb County Naturalist Club is covering reptiles of Cobb County on Tuesday afternoon at the Kennesaw library. Free, 90 minutes, and the kind of thing that sounds random until your kid spends the whole drive home talking about it. These library naturalist sessions are low-key good. Check it out.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for families with younger teens still curious about the natural world, or empty nesters who engage with local community programming. Free and local keeps the barrier low. Recurring series reduces uniqueness, but the specific topic (Cobb County reptiles) gives it enough local flavor to be worth including.

blurb: If you or someone in your house has been noodling around with lyrics and never quite knows what to do with them, this is worth showing up for. Sewell Mill Library is hosting a free monthly songwriting workshop on Tuesday evening, a solid 90 minutes to actually sit down, exercise some creativity, and be around other people who are doing the same thing. It's right up the road, it costs nothing, and you'll walk out with something you didn't have when you walked in. Worth circling.
scoring_notes: Niche event but East Cobb skews educated and creative. Teens and college-age kids home for summer are a natural fit. The local library location and free price point lower the barrier to entry. It's a monthly series so uniqueness is moderate, but it's specific enough to be genuinely useful to the right reader.

blurb: The holiday weekend isn't over yet. C. Ellet's is running their Jazz Brunch on Sunday, which means Southern Fried Chicken, Pancake Paloozas, mimosas, and live jazz from 11am to 3pm while you ease into the rest of your July 4th weekend. It's the Battery, so you're already in a great spot if you want to make a full afternoon of it. Make a reservation before your table's gone.
scoring_notes: C. Ellet's Jazz Brunch runs every Saturday and Sunday, so uniqueness is low. That said, the July 5th timing right after a holiday is a genuine draw for this audience, who likely have family in town or are looking for a low-key but quality outing. Income level makes this a comfortable spend. Knocked down slightly because it's a recurring weekly event.

event_name: Atlanta Braves vs. New York Mets
blurb: The Mets are in town, and if you've been looking for a reason to get out to Truist Park this summer, Monday night is it. There's something about a Braves-Mets matchup that just has a different energy to it, and a July weeknight game with the Battery buzzing is about as good as it gets for a post-holiday outing when the kids are still off and nobody has to be anywhere early. Grab your tickets, get there before first pitch at 7:15, and let someone else do the cooking for once. This one's right in your backyard.
scoring_notes: Braves games are a perennial East Cobb staple. Truist Park is practically in the neighborhood. The Mets are a marquee opponent and a July 4th holiday week game means teens and college kids are home and families are still in vacation mode. Lower uniqueness because it's a recurring home series, but the audience fit is about as strong as it gets.
